title: Evas Strongest Point
wikitext:
[]: Eva's Strongest Point?             Quote Post by light444 » Sat Nov 30, 2013 2:14 am Hi, new here! I watched Eva about a year ago. I liked it's story but not that much. I didnt care but I see lots of ppl rating it as a masterpiece. Well naturally, if something's popular its good, so I thought Im missing something, I mean there must be something they like about Eva, not just its story, but something that makes it a masterpiece. So i saw this recently in MAL http://myanimelist.net/forum/?topicid=692401 . I know theres a reason why Eva is so popular that I even found this site. Anyways, thats how it is. thanks!

[]:             Quote Post by Asukaner117 » Sat Nov 30, 2013 3:25 am For me the strongest point were always the characters. The evangelions, as cool as they may be, are just a plot device, not the main point of the series. That's what separates NGE from most of the other anime's of it's kind. The heroes in NGE are not what you would expect. They are flawed, act selfish, and lack even the most basic skills of human interaction to a level where you feel like yelling at the tv but they are still likeable. You see the reasons why they are acting the way they are, you see the pain and the fear that eats there hearts. And you want them to succeed, you want them to evolve, to live the happy live they deserve. because in the end they are just helpless pawns in a game they dont unterstand, played by powerfull men who fear death more then anything else. You may say that NGE shows you the deep black abbys that lurks in everyone of us. And it shows what happens when you stop to swim against the stream and allow the abbys to win. Of course, that's just my opinion.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Nov 30, 2013 11:43 am I like Eva for the story, the characters, the use of religion and science, and the experimental elements in filmmaking. I mean, when was the last time you remember watching a show where someone talking to their subconscious was expressed by a horizontal line talking to a vertical line?

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Sat Nov 30, 2013 12:00 pm I'd say the absolute strongest points of the series would have to be Anno's genius visual compositions and formidable talent for characterization.

[]:             Quote Post by SimplyMason0 » Sat Nov 30, 2013 12:56 pm The characters and their relationships with how they stick to the Hedgehog's dilemma. Rewatcining it again it feels more like a drama with mecha rather than a mecha with drama like most sci-fi. The Evas and Angels are great and creative.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Nov 30, 2013 5:31 pm Another vote for the characters here -- the only fiction I've encountered where I've encountered a true identification character in the warts and all sense. All that and world-shattering conspiracies too!

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sat Nov 30, 2013 6:16 pm Characterization, definitely. They just feel so... human , especially Shinji. I haven't seen any other show treat so many different aspects of the human condition as skillfully as NGE does.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Nov 30, 2013 6:51 pm Anno's directing comes first, easily, from superb sense of framing to luxurious editing. Then rich characterization, daring and brilliantly deviant narrative structure third.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Nov 30, 2013 7:16 pm Getting repetitive - but it's characterisation for me as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Princess Asuka » Sat Nov 30, 2013 8:50 pm The characters, I can easily relate to Shinji and Asuka. I thought, oh it's gonna be a typical giant robot anime, I watched it and boy was I wrong.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Sat Nov 30, 2013 8:55 pm I agree with the five previous posters in regards to the characterization. Also, Evangelion was the first anime that brought out my inner little boy.......and masculinity.....I'll leave it to your imagination by what I mean. It being a lolmecha anime is another factor too.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Nov 30, 2013 10:49 pm Characterization, richness of setting, direction, and the way everything is so thickly interwoven with everything else.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Nov 30, 2013 11:41 pm The rest of this thread wrote: Characterization plot direction creativity blah blah blah

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Sun Dec 01, 2013 1:39 am Xard wrote: luxurious editing

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Sun Dec 01, 2013 4:19 am Almost everything in Evangelion is amazing. IMO NGE's strongest point=Characters. NME's strongest point= Plot.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Sun Dec 01, 2013 4:35 am Rebuild 's strongest point is SPLOSIONS

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Sun Dec 01, 2013 7:21 pm gatotsu911 wrote: Rebuild 's strongest point is SPLOSIONS

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Sun Dec 01, 2013 7:34 pm Xard wrote: Anno's directing comes first, easily, from superb sense of framing to luxurious editing. Then rich characterization, daring and brilliantly deviant narrative structure third.

[]:             Quote Post by Sgt. Griff » Sun Dec 01, 2013 8:13 pm I would say the Core is probably an Eva's strongest point.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sun Dec 01, 2013 9:26 pm Mark me down for characterization I guess. Really hard to pick just one thing when what makes Eva so great is all of it combined, though. Sgt. Griff wrote: I would say the Core is probably an Eva's strongest point.

Well naturally, if something's popular its good, so I thought Im missing something, I mean there must be something they like about Eva, not just its story, but something that makes it a masterpiece.
